steps.title: Locate the structural contrast
steps.content: The passage explicitly contrasts ferritic and austenitic stainless steel: “Unlike ferritic stainless steel, austenitic stainless steel has a face-centered cubic crystalline structure resulting from the addition of nickel.” Storage containers use the 300 series, a subtype of austenitic steel, making this structural difference the key to the answer.

steps.title: Match the structure to the application
steps.content:
Storage containers use 300 series austenitic steel, which has the face-centered cubic crystalline structure described in the passage.

Induction cookers use ferritic steel, explicitly contrasted as having a different structure.

The other choices contradict the passage by reversing relationships or attributing unsupported properties.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
